ODBCMaxConnections.description: Maximum number of simultaneous ODBC connections across all sources combined. Connections beyond this limit trigger LRU eviction of the least-recently-used handle. Default is 10.

ODBCSources.description: Associative array of ODBC data source configurations. Each key is a source ID, each value is an array with keys: dsn, user, password, driver (optional), server (optional), database (optional), options (optional), prepared (optional for named prepared statements).

ODBCAllowArbitraryQueries.description: If true, wiki editors with odbc-query permission can pass arbitrary SQL. If false, only prepared statements defined in ODBCSources are allowed. Note: Individual sources can override this with the 'allow_queries' setting.

ODBCMaxRows.description: Maximum number of rows returned by any single ODBC query.

ODBCCacheExpiry.description: Number of seconds to cache query results. 0 means no caching. When enabled, query results are cached per unique SQL+parameters+maxRows combination. Cache is automatically invalidated on expiry; there is no manual invalidation. Choose expiry time based on how stale data can be.

ODBCExternalDataIntegration.description: If true and External Data extension is installed, register as a connector for External Data. Must be set before extension loading.

ODBCMaxQueriesPerPage.description: Maximum number of {{#odbc_query:}} calls allowed per page render. 0 means no limit (default, for backward compatibility). Set to a positive integer to prevent resource exhaustion from excessive per-page queries. Exceeding the limit returns an error from the offending call; earlier calls on the same page are unaffected.

ODBCSlowQueryThreshold.description: Execution time in seconds above which a query is logged to the 'odbc-slow' log channel. 0 means disabled (default). Set to a positive float, e.g. 2.0, to enable slow-query logging. Query timing covers odbc_execute() + all row fetches. Requires the 'odbc-slow' channel to be configured in $wgDebugLogGroups.
